half_dome.day_hiker_permit: March lottery, with a limited number of permits available two days in advance.
safety_notes: Do not attempt the cables in rain, thunderstorms, or when the rock surface is wet.
Bring proper footwear, gloves, enough water, and a real flashlight or headlamp.
This hike is not appropriate for unprepared hikers, people uncomfortable with exposure, or people without the endurance for a very long day.

core_facts: Yosemite has 13 popular campgrounds on reservation systems from April through October.
Only Upper Pines Campground is on reservations all year.
Upper Pines, Lower Pines, and North Pines are Yosemite Valley options released five months in advance, with North Pines using a 2026 early access lottery.
Camp 4 is a Yosemite Valley campground with reservations one week in advance in the main season and first-come, first-served in winter.

reasons: No Half Dome permit is listed in the source JSON.
The group is only comfortable with 5-6 hour hikes, while Half Dome is typically a 10-12 hour day.
The group lacks gloves for the cables, enough headlamps, and enough water capacity.
One traveler is anxious with exposed heights.
trip_assessment.secondary_note: Camp 4 is a reasonable budget / backpacker-style fallback but is less ideal for a first Yosemite car-camping trip.
